summary: Frontend-only testing completed for the new desktop paths camera-flyby + mobile stepper behavior on /app/refgd homepage. Core requested desktop/mobile rendering and scroll behavior passed, but one linked route is broken.

integration_issues.flow: Desktop path card navigation (card 1: Refund Store List)
integration_issues.issue: Navigating to /store-list opens Next.js runtime error page (500) due missing DB env vars (RENDER_DATABASE_URL or DATABASE_URL).

action_items: Fix /store-list route runtime error by providing required DB env config or adding graceful fallback for missing DB in preview/dev.
Re-verify homepage card-1 navigation after /store-list route fix.
Optional: reduce constant card motion during click tests if QA automation stability is needed (Playwright reports links as continuously unstable due animation).
critical_code_review_comments: PathsHorizontalReveal desktop branch and mobile branch are correctly split by breakpoint; requested camera flyby structure is present and transforms with scroll.
Console shows repeated SVG attribute errors (x1/x2/cx/cy undefined) on /exclusive-mentorships page; unrelated to this request but should be cleaned up.
Missing /app/memory/test_credentials.md in workspace (not needed for this non-auth run, but required by process for auth testing).

success_rate.frontend: 80% (4/5 requested behaviors passed; one affected by broken linked route)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Desktop checks passed for flyby existence, 5-card visibility, transform-on-scroll, and no horizontal overflow. Mobile checks passed for stepper-only rendering, first-card initial visibility, vertical one-step progression (when centered in section), and no sideways-step regression. Blocker found: /store-list target route throws runtime 500.
rca of the issue: Repro: open homepage desktop -> click card 1 (path-card-1-link) or navigate directly to /store-list. Observed Next runtime error with message 'No database URL found. Set RENDER_DATABASE_URL (Render) or DATABASE_URL (Neon/other Postgres)'. Root cause appears to be missing env configuration in db pool initialization for that route. Mitigation: set required DB env var in runtime environment or harden route with fallback/error boundary when DB is unavailable.
